## [00:00 - 03:03]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Here's how medic could remove audience suggestions. Let's go. A few weeks ago I published an episode where I talked about how audience suggestions are more hassle than they're worth. Advertisers often don't realize their inputs are only suggestions. And they'll make suggestions by age range or gender and get mad when ads are shown outside of those groups, or they'll suggest custom audiences and think they're remarketing when they're not. And now that detailed targeting and look-like audiences are only suggestions for most performance goals, it's questionable how valuable detailed targeting and look-likes are. The biggest problem right now is the confusion that suggestions cause. Advertisers either think their suggestions are tight constraints or assume their suggestions do more than they actually do. It results in anger and frustration and conspiracy theories that end up spilling over to location targeting and exclusions. Meta respects location targeting and exclusions as audience controls their never suggestions. But you'll have a hard time convincing advertisers of that who are confused and frustrated by suggestions. So my proposal isn't to remove all targeting inputs. Instead, we should remove audience suggestions so that we can stop pretending that they do anything. Suggestions present the illusion of control. They give advertisers what they think is another lever, when there's little evidence it makes an impact. So here's what I envision. Now think back to the old days of Advantage Plus shopping campaigns. The setup back then was completely stripped down. You could define a location, but that was it. The way this could work would be something in between those old Advantage Plus shopping campaign days and the current mess that we have now. By default, provide only audience controls. These allow you to define your location and exclude custom audiences if necessary. No suggestions related to age, gender, custom audiences, look like audiences or detailed targeting. Fewer opportunities for confusion. And you're usually better off not touching these things anyway. But unlike the old Advantage Plus shopping campaigns, keep that button to further limit the reach of your ads. When clicked, you would turn Advantage Plus off in exchange for access to some targeting restrictions. But these aren't suggestions. We've eliminated them. The only options available would be the only options that we have now without suggestions. So if you can't restrict by detailed targeting

or look like audience, those fields are grayed out. And right now, you can't restrict by detailed targeting when using 11 performance goals. And you'll still be able to restrict by custom audience and do actual remarketing, not just suggestion remarketing. Once again, I wouldn't necessarily recommend remarketing in most cases these days because meta prioritizes those groups automatically. But it would still be available. So here's the bottom of the glass. While this sounds like a dramatic change, it's not. It simply stops pretending like you have control that you don't. If you previously had the ability to restrict by a certain audience type, you would still be able to do that. But the concept of a suggestion would be gone. Now it would freak advertisers out who falsely believe those suggestions did more than they did. But it will also prevent them from making mistakes based on these false assumptions.
